 {"id":"bd-808","title":"Create scripts/test_local_git.sh","description":"**Integration tests with local git repos**

## What
Test script that creates local git repos and tests operations against them.

## Why
We can't rely on network for CI tests. Local repos let us test all scenarios deterministically.

## Test Scenarios
```bash
# Setup
TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap "rm -rf $TEMP_DIR" EXIT

# Create 'remote' bare repo
git init --bare "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git"

# Clone and make commits
git clone "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git" "$TEMP_DIR/local"
cd "$TEMP_DIR/local"
echo "initial" > file.txt
git add . && git commit -m "Initial"
git push origin main

# Create 'projects dir' clone
git clone "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git" "$TEMP_DIR/projects/testrepo"

# Make new commit in 'remote' (via local)
echo "update" >> "$TEMP_DIR/local/file.txt"
git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" add . && git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" commit -m "Update"
git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" push

# Test: status should show 'behind'
# Test: pull should update
# Test: dirty detection
# Test: diverged detection
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run without network
- All status conditions tested
- CI can run this script","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:43:05.77151921-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:43:05.77151921-05:00","close_reason":"Created test_local_git.sh with 17 passing tests for git operations (is_git_repo, status detection, do_pull)","labels":["git","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-802","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.156263651-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.186539707-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.216416421-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-809","title":"Handle branch not found gracefully","description":"**Graceful handling when repo@branch branch doesn't exist**

## What
When user specifies repo@branch and branch doesn't exist, handle gracefully.

## Why
Branches get deleted, renamed, or typos happen. Current plan doesn't address this.

## Behavior
```
$ ru sync owner/repo@nonexistent
Cloning: owner/repo
  Warning: Branch 'nonexistent' not found
  Falling back to default branch (main)
```

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    # ... clone repo ...
    
    if [[ -n "$branch" ]]; then
        if git -C "$target_dir" rev-parse --verify "origin/$branch" &>/dev/null; then
            git -C "$target_dir" checkout "$branch" --quiet
        else
            log_warn "Branch '$branch' not found, using default branch"
            write_result "$repo_name" "clone" "branch_not_found" "" "Branch: $branch"
        fi
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clone succeeds with warning
- Default branch used as fallback
- Result recorded for summary
- User informed clearly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:59:41.810187043-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:59:41.810187043-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented graceful branch-not-found handling with warning and fallback to default branch","labels":["error-handling","git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-809","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.797824099-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-809","depends_on_id":"bd-902","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.828864235-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-810","title":"Handle network timeouts gracefully","description":"**Handle network timeouts gracefully**

## What
Add timeout handling for clone and pull operations.

## Why
Flaky networks, slow connections, or overloaded servers can cause operations to hang indefinitely. Users need a way to bound wait time.

## Implementation
- Set GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT=1000 and GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME=30 as defaults
- These cause git to abort if transfer rate drops below 1KB/s for 30 seconds
- Add --timeout N option to override (sets GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME)
- On timeout, log error and continue to next repo
- Report timeouts distinctly in summary (not just 'failed')

## Acceptance Criteria
- Slow operations time out after reasonable period
- Timeout errors are clearly reported
- Other repos continue processing after timeout
- Timeout can be configured via flag or config","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T17:01:47.558978485-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T17:01:47.558978485-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented network timeout handling with GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_* env vars, --timeout flag, and timeout detection in clone/pull","labels":["resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.921445046-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.952318959-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.981841826-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
